text.title: cicd-tls-renewal-automation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for cicd-tls-renewal-automation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: HTTP-01 challenge selected when the pipeline later needs wildcard certificates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: HTTP-01 cannot issue wildcard certs. Flag wildcard requests early and force DNS-01 from the start — migrating mid-flight requires re-issuance and potential downtime.

failure-mode.description: Iterating on certificate issuance against the production LE endpoint during development.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Let's Encrypt rate limits: 50 certs/week per registered domain. Agents or pipelines iterating on issuance burn the quota fast. Always use the staging endpoint (https://acme-staging-v02.api.letsencrypt.org/directory) during development and testing.

failure-mode.description: Defaulting to RSA-2048 key generation when ECDSA P-256 is available.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ECDSA P-256 is faster (smaller signatures, faster handshakes) and the modern standard. RSA-2048 provides 112-bit security while P-256 provides 128-bit security at a fraction of the computational cost.

failure-mode.description: ARI (ACME Renewal Information) checks disabled or ignored.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: ARI lets the CA signal early renewal (e.g., due to mass revocation events). Ignoring ARI means your automation misses CA-recommended renewal windows and certs may expire unexpectedly during a CA incident.
